titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Richard Gamble Knight Papers
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Suzanne Noruschat and Christine Connolly

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 10:25

did.abstract: The papers comprise images captured during Richard Gamble Knight's tenure from 1957 to 1961 as an employee of the architectural firm Eero Saarinen and Associates, and during other periods of Knight's life, such as his student years. Consisting of photographic prints, negatives, and slides, the images range from projects designed by Saarinen and Associates (including Dulles International Airport, John Deere and Company, Trans World Flight Center, and CBS Building in New York), to candid shots of Saarinen and his employees at work and socializing, to photographic studies of architectural forms and details. Also included in the papers are audio recordings of interviews Knight conducted with five former Saarinen employees for his book, Saarinen's Quest: A Memoir , published in 2008.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The records are arranged in two series: I. Images: prints, negatives, and slides, circa 1952-circa 1979. II. Interviews conducted for Saarinen's Quest: A Memoir , 2001-2003.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Richard Gamble Knight Papers (MS 1999). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has been transferred to Yale University for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creator of this collection. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers comprise images captured during Richard Gamble Knight's tenure from 1957 to 1961 as an employee of the architectural firm Eero Saarinen and Associates, and during other periods of Knight's life, such as his student years. Consisting of photographic prints, negatives, and slides, the images range from projects designed by Saarinen and Associates (including Dulles International Airport, John Deere and Company, Trans World Flight Center, CBS Building in New York, and Morse and Stiles Colleges at Yale University), to candid shots of Saarinen and his employees at work and socializing, to photographic studies of architectural forms and details. Also included in the papers are audio recordings of interviews Knight conducted with five former Saarinen employees for his book, Saarinen's Quest: A Memoir , published in 2008.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Born in Flint, Michigan, on November 29, 1926, Richard Gamble Knight studied engineering and architecture at the University of Michigan (BS Engineering, 1951), the Cranbrook Academy of Art, and Lawrence Technical University (BS Architecture, 1957) in Southfield, Michigan. In 1957, Knight was hired as a junior architectural designer at Eero Saarinen and Associates in Bloomfield Hills, Michigan, where he worked until Saarinen's death in 1961. A budding photographer as well as an architect, Knight succeeded Balthazar Korab as the firm's "court photographer," documenting the design process for clients and creating a visual record of the firm's activities and creative output. After Saarinen's death in 1961, Knight continued his career as an architect, and also took on roles as a graphic and interior designer, an instructor of design, a writer, and a prolific multimedia artist whose works were exhibited regularly. In 2008, Knight published Saarinen's Quest: A Memoir (San Francisco: William Stout Publishers), which focuses on Knight's time at Eero Saarinen and Associates, and features many of the photographs Knight took while employed at the firm. Knight died on December 16, 2008, in Alameda, California.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy of non-commercially produced items for their personal use should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

corpname.part: Trans World Airlines Terminal (New York, N.Y.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Images of the CBS Building, Jefferson National Expansion Memorial (St. Louis Gateway Arch), TWA terminal in New York, U.S. Embassy in London, and Yale University's Morse and Stiles residential colleges and the David Ingalls Rink.

did.unittitle: CBS Building, New York, New York
General Motors Technical Center, Warren, Michigan
International Business Machines (IBM), Rochester, Minnesotta, and Yorktown, New York
Jefferson National Expansion Museum, St. Louis, Missouri

odd.head: General
odd.p: This project is commonly referred to as the St. Louis Gateway Arch.

did.unittitle: John Deere and Company Administrative Headquarters, Moline, Illinois
Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) Chapel and Kresge Auditorium, Cambridge, Massachusetts
Miller, J. Irwin, residence, Columbus, Indiana
Milwaukee County War Memorial, Milwaukee, Wisconsin
Trans World Flight Center (TWA Terminal 5), JFK Airport, New York, New York
University of Pennsylvania, Women's Residence Hall, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
Vivian Beaumont Repertory Theater and Library Museum, New York, New York
New Colleges (Samuel F. B. Morse and Ezra Stiles Colleges)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unidentified images in this series may have been projects Knight pursued as a student.

did.unittitle: Illustrations for Saarinen's Quest: A Memoir
Interviews conducted for Saarinen's Quest: A Memoir

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Interview at his office, 1056 Chapel Street, New Haven, Connecticut.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Interview at his office, Roth & Moore Architects, 65 Audubon Street, New Haven, Connecticut.
